management_notes:
Artemis Gold's management team, led by CEO and Director Steven Dean along with an operationally focused executive team, delivered one of the most successful junior-to-producer transitions in recent Canadian mining history. Commercial production at the Blackwater Gold Mine was declared May 1, 2025 — on schedule and budget — following first gold pour in January 2025. Full-year 2025 production of 192,808 oz Au demonstrated operational competence immediately, culminating in a record Q4 of 68,480 oz. The team also shepherded the Phase 2 (EP2) expansion decision — a $1 billion commitment to grow throughput from 8 Mtpa to 21 Mtpa by 2028 — targeting 500,000 to 525,000 oz/year average for the first 10 full years of Phase 2 operation.

Insider alignment is substantial. Individual insiders hold shares with a reported value of approximately CA$211 million at late 2025 prices, with private equity backing (Orion Mine Finance) holding a strategic anchor stake. Insider buying has exceeded selling over the trailing 12 months. The management team's track record — delivering a large-scale, low-cost, open-pit gold mine in British Columbia's tier-1 jurisdiction — is among the strongest in the junior-to-emerging-producer space.

The Q4 2025 earnings call revealed adjusted net income of $145.8 million ($0.61/share) and full-year 2025 adjusted EBITDA of $610.4 million on revenue of $913.9 million. Cash flow from operations was $560.7 million for the year. This level of financial performance validates the team's execution capability.
geology_notes:
Blackwater is one of the largest undeveloped gold deposits in Canada by reserve ounces, and is now a producing mine. The Definitive Feasibility Study supports Proven and Probable mineral reserves of 8.0 million oz Au contained within 334 million tonnes grading 0.75 g/t Au — the highest reserve classification attainable under NI 43-101. Total Measured and Indicated resources stand at 11.7 million oz Au (597 Mt at 0.61 g/t), with a further resource update expected in 2026. The deposit is a bulk-tonnage, low-strip-ratio open pit in the Nechako Plateau of central British Columbia, a proven and politically stable mining jurisdiction.

The resource has been de-risked to the highest possible study tier. Proven and Probable reserves are supported by a Definitive Feasibility Study (DFS). The reserve conversion rate is high (8.0 Moz P&P from a larger M&I base). The deposit style — disseminated gold in a volcanic-hosted setting — is amenable to bulk mining and heap-leach style processing, with strong recoveries confirmed in production. No significant metallurgical surprises have emerged in the first year of operation.

The geological upside is also meaningful: 3.7 Moz additional M&I resource exists beyond the current reserve envelope, plus Inferred material. An updated reserve/resource statement is expected in 2026. Blackwater qualifies as a tier-1 deposit on both size and jurisdiction. Score: 5/5.
capital_notes:
As of December 31, 2025, Artemis held cash and equivalents of $168.1 million and total available liquidity of $410.1 million. Subsequent to year-end, the company closed a $450 million offering of 5.625% senior unsecured five-year notes, bringing pro-forma available liquidity to $852.7 million — well-capitalised to fund the Phase 2 expansion. Operating cash flow for full-year 2025 was $560.7 million, sufficient to self-fund Phase 1A construction and contribute meaningfully to Phase 2.

Share count increased from 211 million (end FY2024) to approximately 232 million by Q3 2025, a ~10% increase in nine months, attributable to stock option exercises and equity compensation. This is moderate dilution for a company in production ramp-up. Fully diluted count will need monitoring as Phase 2 proceeds. The $450 million debt at 5.625% is manageable relative to $610 million EBITDA but is a leverage factor to watch if gold prices retreat sharply.

The Phase 2 expansion capex is approximately $1.0 billion and is expected to be funded through a combination of operating cash flows and the bond proceeds. This is a significant capital commitment, but Blackwater's cost structure (lowest-decile AISC) and strong FCF generation at current gold prices of ~$4,600 USD/oz make it fundable without additional equity dilution. Capital risk: moderate but manageable. Score: 4/5.
catalyst_notes:
The near-term and medium-term catalyst schedule is dense and high-impact. Phase 1A throughput expansion (to 8 Mtpa) is on track for completion before end of 2026, which will lift annual production toward ~250,000-300,000 oz/year from the Phase 1 level of ~200,000 oz. This alone represents a 25-50% production step-up within 12 months.

Phase 2 (EP2) approval is already secured — the $1 billion expansion to 21 Mtpa throughput is now in execution. First EP2 ore through the expanded plant is targeted before end of 2028, lifting Blackwater to 500,000-525,000 oz/year average for the first 10 full years of that phase. This transforms Artemis from a mid-tier junior producer into a significant intermediate gold company. A mineral reserve/resource update in 2026 will also serve as a catalyst, likely adding ounces given continued infill drilling success.

With gold trading at approximately $4,637 USD/oz as of late April 2026 — versus the DFS base case gold price — every near-term milestone delivers significantly more economic value than was modelled at construction-go decision. The combination of production growth (Phase 1A), transformational expansion (Phase 2), and a high-gold-price operating environment creates one of the strongest near-term catalyst profiles in the sector.
acquisition_notes:
Valuing Artemis Gold requires a producer framework rather than a junior P/NAV model, as Blackwater is now operating. At April 2026 stock price of ~$37.78 CAD and approximately 232 million shares outstanding, market capitalisation is ~$8.77 billion CAD. Phase 1 annual FCF (at $4,637 USD/oz gold, ~$1,000 CAD/oz AISC) is roughly CA$700-800 million/year; Phase 2 will push this toward CA$1.5-1.8 billion/year at steady state. Using a 5% discount rate and Phase 1 + Phase 2 combined NPV, an estimated intrinsic NAV of approximately CA$45/share is supportable at current gold prices — based on FS-level P&P reserves and confirmed production economics. This gives an implied P/NAV of approximately 0.84x.

The FS-level reserve base (8.0 Moz P&P, highest confidence classification) supports taking the NAV estimate at face value. The market has not yet re-rated Artemis to reflect Phase 2 at current gold prices, leaving a meaningful gap between current market cap and estimated intrinsic value. Comparable acquisitions of producing British Columbia gold mines have transacted at premiums to current reserve values in 2024-2026.

The key constraint on score is the $450 million of senior notes and the remaining Phase 2 capex obligation, which reduce free cash available to equity holders during the construction period (2026-2028). Adjusted P/NAV of ~0.84x on FS-level economics at current gold prices = 4/5. This is a producing company, not a junior — the 5/5 threshold (0.3x P/NAV) is unlikely to be reached for an operating asset this size.
analyst_summary:
Artemis Gold earns a BUY with a composite score of 23/25, driven by perfect marks on management execution and project geology. Blackwater is now a producing, tier-1 gold mine in British Columbia, delivering 192,808 oz Au in its first full year of commercial production (declared May 2025). The management team's track record — on-budget, on-schedule, and now approved for a $1 billion Phase 2 expansion targeting 500,000-525,000 oz/year — is among the strongest in the Canadian gold space. The project is underpinned by 8.0 million oz Proven and Probable reserves (Definitive Feasibility Study level, highest classification), with an additional 3.7 Moz M&I resource providing further upside.

The main risks are the $450 million senior note obligation and the Phase 2 capex execution burden over 2026-2028, which constrain near-term free cash flow to equity. Share count has grown ~10% in nine months. These factors prevent a 5/5 capital score. At current gold prices (~$4,637 USD/oz vs DFS assumption), the intrinsic value of the asset is dramatically higher than it was at construction decision, but this is already partially reflected in the ~$8.77 billion market cap. The estimated P/NAV of ~0.84x on FS-level economics at current gold prices represents a discount that will widen further as Phase 2 production ramps.

Key catalyst to watch: Phase 1A throughput expansion completion (end 2026) and first EP2 production through the expanded 21 Mtpa plant (targeted end 2028). A resource/reserve update in 2026 could materially increase the stated reserve base. Retail investors should note this has already transitioned from a junior to an emerging mid-tier producer — the risk profile is meaningfully lower than it was 24 months ago.
